title: Payments API Retry Storm
description: Infinite retry recursion in payments-service PR #381 has triggered an aggressive retry storm, exhausting the PostgreSQL connection pool.
brief: Payment retries are overwhelming the database after a failed deployment.
root_cause: An infinite retry recursion bug in PR #381 caused upstream gateway threads to mass-retry connections to the database, saturating pg_stat slots.
initial_logs: WARNING: postgres-statefulset: Connection pool nearing capacity (85/100).
ERROR: redis-cache: Unable to allocate memory for sorted set operation.
WARNING: auth-deployment: Increased latency observed in token validation service.
evolving_logs: ERROR: postgres-statefulset: Connection pool exhausted (100/100). Retries failing.
CRITICAL: redis-cache: OOMKilled - Container terminated due to memory pressure.
ERROR: redis-cache: Pod Evicted due to NodeNotReady - Insufficient resources.
ERROR: auth-deployment: CrashLoopBackOff - Token service failing to initialize.
CRITICAL: postgres-statefulset: High query latency impacting upstream services.
WARNING: redis-cache: Persistent Redis cache failure causing upstream retries.

postgres-statefulset.saturation:
FATAL: remaining connection slots are reserved for non-replication superuser connections
LOG:  could not accept new connection: Too many open files

auth-deployment.timeout:
Error: connect ETIMEDOUT 10.42.0.12:5432
at TCPConnectWrap.afterConnect [as oncomplete] (net.js:1146:16)

redis-cache.exhaustion: OOM command not allowed when used memory > 'maxmemory'.

sandbox.template:
# Kubernetes Recovery Console
kubectl get pods -n production
